input.background:
For several makes of car, data for engine size and fuel economy are shown below:

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Make & Engine size(l) & Fuel economy ( $\mathrm{kml}^{-1}$ ) & Make & Engine size(l) & Fuel economy ( $\mathrm{kml}^{-1}$ ) & Make & Engine size (l) & Fuel economy ( $\mathrm{km} \mathrm{l}^{-1}$ ) \\
\hline A & 1.0 & 14.3 & I & 1.1 & 14.9 & Q & 1.6 & 12.0 \\
\hline B & 1.0 & 16.4 & J & 1.4 & 12.7 & R & 1.6 & 12.4 \\
\hline C & 1.0 & 16.7 & K & 1.4 & 12.8 & S & 1.6 & 12.5 \\
\hline D & 1.0 & 17.2 & L & 1.4 & 13.5 & T & 1.6 & 12.8 \\
\hline E & 1.0 & 17.6 & M & 1.4 & 13.9 & U & 1.6 & 13.9 \\
\hline F & 1.0 & 17.6 & N & 1.4 & 14.5 & V & 1.6 & 14.7 \\
\hline G & 1.0 & 18.2 & O & 1.4 & 15.2 & W & 1.8 & 11.5 \\
\hline H & 1.1 & 14.9 & P & 1.4 & 15.6 & X & 1.8 & 12.8 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
input.question: Plot the residuals against either the independent variable or the fitted values of $y$.

output.answer: Residuals plotted against or Residuals plotted against the independent variable fitted values of $y$.

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
